title: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple
tagline: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple (also known as Kattampack Kizhakkum Bhagam Pattupurackal Bhagavathy temple) is an ancient (dating back to the 10th century CE) Bhadrakali temple loc
overview.description: Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple (also known as Kattampack Kizhakkum Bhagam Pattupurackal Bhagavathy temple) is an ancient (dating back to the 10th century CE) Bhadrakali temple located in Njeezhoor village, Kottayam, Kerala. It is the only temple in Njeezhoor village where the presiding deity is Bhadrakali. Offering naranga vilakku (Lemon lamp) and neivilakku (Ghee lamp) on every Tuesday and Friday is very significant in this temple. The nearest towns are Kuravilangad and Kaduthuruthy.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (42 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pattupurackal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Marangoly, Njeezhoor, Achickal, Monippally, Elanji.
